title: Circles and arcs from the midpoints of four sides

rule.instruction: Circles and arcs from the midpoints of four sides. (ACG 59)

restrictions: Faithful base (circles + arcs swung from the four SIDE MIDPOINTS, per the instruction) executed through the organic lens — but this round deliberately attacks the ROUND-13 lesson: jitter roughens texture, not structure, so hand-drawn wobble alone left #130's arcs 'still showing symmetric'. Here the arrangement's symmetry is broken via the new arcs levers: centreJitter (midpoints wander off the tidy cross; some pull inward -> true CIRCLES, edge ones stay ARCS), countJitter (each family gets an uneven number), radiusJitter (irregular, non-even concentric spacing). Monochrome black on white. Never crisp (always some hand). The 16 span offset strength, family count (4 midpoints vs +corners), reach (near vs full sweep), spacing irregularity, and hand energy.
